# Project Plan

<!-- Generated by /init — defines the development lifecycle -->

## Current Phase

**POC → MVP.** Greenfield, solo, tightly scoped, ship-quickly. The approach is
**de-risk the data first**, then build the smallest app that delivers a correct
verdict, then automate updates. The barcode→brand matching is the only genuinely
unknown part, so it is proven before any UI investment.

**v1 "done" = a working TestFlight build** running on the developer's own iPhone.
Public App Store release is explicitly a *later* goal, not part of v1 (see
Long-term Plan).

## Milestones

### Milestone 0 — Data Spike (POC)
Manually run the data pipeline once: pull Nestlé's brand list from Wikidata
(`Q160746`) + Wikipedia, filter the Open Food Facts dump to Nestlé-associated
barcodes, and build the SQLite (brand→parent + barcodes tables).
**Done when:** scanning/looking up a real Nestlé product barcode (e.g.,
KitKat, Nescafé) resolves to "Nestlé" in a throwaway test. This validates the
whole concept and the brand-name normalization approach.

### Milestone 1 — Core App (MVP)
SwiftUI scan view (VisionKit) → on-device SQLite lookup → verdict
(Yes / No / Unknown) + ownership chain. Bundled DB, local manual brand-search
fallback. **Minimal** theme. Accessibility baseline (VoiceOver, Dynamic Type,
no color-only verdicts).
**Done when:** the app runs on the developer's phone and returns correct
verdicts fully offline.

### Milestone 2 — Self-Update + Polish + Coverage (→ v1 / TestFlight)
Automate the GitHub Actions daily pipeline → GitHub Releases (multi-source: OFF +
Open Beauty Facts + Wikidata + curated); implement the in-app daily manifest check
+ dataset download/swap; add the **opt-in online fallback** (off by default, free
OFF API) so the app can cover **any** barcode and give confident "not Nestlé"
verdicts; add the **Informational** theme + settings (theme picker, online-lookup
toggle); add ODbL attribution + accuracy disclaimer; publish the privacy policy on
GitHub Pages.
**Done when:** a **TestFlight** build is installed and working on the developer's
iPhone, updating its dataset daily — **this is the v1 goal.**

## Long-term Plan

After the v1 TestFlight build is working:

- **Public App Store release** — App Store submission with the "Data Not Collected"
  privacy label (requires the Apple Developer account + Xcode Cloud release flow).
- **Planned features** (see `FEATURES.md`) — scan history, share results,
  non-target alternatives, multi-company boycott lists, "avoid company X" export.
- **Generalize beyond Nestlé** — add other parent companies as data (new
  `parent`/`is_target` rows) and let the user select active boycott targets.
- **More themes** — the theme system (see `UI.md`) is additive; new presentations
  can be added without touching core logic.

This is intended as an ongoing, maintained project (the dataset refreshes daily),
not a one-off build.
